text.title: mvp-scoping Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mvp-scoping as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every item added to Must-Have extends the timeline and narrows the learning. With 10 Must-Haves, the MVP is a 3-month project. Cap at 5 and demote the rest.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without explicit exclusions, scope creep enters through "just one more thing" requests. The Won't-Have list is a public commitment that prevents renegotiation of every cut feature.

failure-mode.description: No learning goal or kill signal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An MVP without a hypothesis is just a small product launch. Without a pre-agreed failure criterion, teams ship regardless of results and call it validated.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If it takes 6 months, it is not minimum. Revisit scope, cut Must-Haves further, or use micro-MVPs to validate assumptions before building.

failure-mode.description: Building MVP without weekly user feedback.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Assumptions invalidated at week 2 can redirect the remaining build. Waiting until launch to discover the hypothesis is wrong wastes all remaining build time.
